'''Brett Delaney''' (born 26 October 1985) is an Australian [[rugby league]] coach and former professional rugby league footballer who was most recently assistant coach for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He played as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[Super League]] and for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[NRL]].

==Background==
Delaney was born in [[Baulkham Hills, New South Wales|Baulkham Hills]], [[New South Wales]], Australia.

==Career==
===Parramatta Eels===
Delaney began his professional career with the [[Parramatta Eels]]. He scored a try on his first grade début against [[St George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] in 2005 as the club won the [[Minor Premiership]] that year. He went on to make 18 appearances in total for the [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] club, scoring four tries.  His final game for Parramatta was their 12-6 qualifying final loss to [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/about/official-player-numbers/|title=Official Player Numbers|website=Parramatta Eels}}</ref>

===Gold Coast Titans===
After limited opportunities at the [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], Delaney joined the newly formed club the [[Gold Coast Titans]] for the [[2007 NRL season]]. Delaney scored 14 tries in 62 games during his three seasons with the [[Gold Coast Titans|Titans]]. In 2009, his final season at the club, he helped the team finish in 3rd place in the regular season ladder, which meant the [[Gold Coast Titans]] qualified for the play-offs for the first time. Unfortunately for Delaney, a [[hamstring]] injury meant he missed the end of the season. He watched from the stands as the Gold Coast crashed out of the play-offs, 27–2 to eventual finalists, & former club, Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2017/08/24/throwback--the-2009-run-to-a-grand-final/|title=THROWBACK The 2009 run to a grand final|website=www.parraeels.com.au}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
Delaney signed a three-year contract with the [[Leeds Rhinos]], starting in 2010. He was signed as a replacement for [[Rugby Union]] bound [[Lee Smith (rugby league)|Lee Smith]], who had signed a contract with the [[Wasps RFC|London Wasps]].

Delaney made his unofficial début for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in [[Matt Diskin]]'s Testimonial Game at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] against the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Delaney scored the match winning try in a 12–10 victory.  Delaney's official début came on the opening night of the 2010 [[Super League]] season against the [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]]. He scored a try in the 34–6 victory. Delaney made 30 appearances & scored 10 tries during his first season at the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club.

He played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live }}</ref>

New [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] coach [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] started Delaney in the back-row for the first time in his [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] career in the second game of the 2011 season away against [[Hull F.C.]] Delaney impressed in his new role but unfortunately suffered an ankle injury which ruled him out for approximately six weeks. Delaney returned from injury and continued to impress in the back row. That year 2011 Delaney went on to win his first Grand final for Leeds playing at second-row forward. His first three years saw him play in three successive [[Challenge Cup]] finals (2010, 2011 and 2012) but Leeds lost all three to Warrington (twice) and Wigan.

He played in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Brown|first=Oliver|title=Challenge Cup final: Leeds Rhinos 18 Wigan Warriors 28|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8727501/Challenge-Cup-final-Leeds-Rhinos-18-Wigan-Warriors-28.html|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=The Telegraph|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Henson|first=Mike|title=Challenge Cup final – as it happened|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9574171.stm|access-date=21 November 2019|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref>

Delaney played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=The Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=The Guardian|location=UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
}}</ref>
Delaney played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>

Delaney played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2015, the Leeds side made history by going on to win the treble (the [[League Leaders' Shield]], the Challenge Cup Final<ref name="Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/aug/29/leeds-emphatically-shut-out-hull-kr-to-win-challenge-cup|title=Leeds emphatically shut out Hull KR to lift Challenge Cup|work=The Guardian|date=29 August 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Scoreboard |newspaper=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=2982 |date=31 August 2015 |page=31}}</ref><ref name=BBC2908>{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34086760 |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 0-50 Leeds Rhinos |publisher=BBC Sport |date=29 August 2015 |access-date=29 August 2015}}</ref> and Grand Final).
He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=The Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

A facial injury sustained while playing in July 2018 required surgery and at the end of the 2018 season Delaney left Leeds to join Championship side [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] for whom he was set to play in the [[2019 RFL Championship|2019 Championship]]. However a reoccurrence of the injury necessitating further surgery in a pre-season friendly against Halifax prompted Delaney to announce an end to his playing career in February 2019.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.totalrl.com/multiple-super-league-winner-brett-delaney-calls-time-on-playing-career/ |title=Multiple Super League winner Brett Delaney calls time on playing career |publisher=Total RL |date=6 February 2019 |access-date=6 February 2019}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 7 February 2019, Delaney announced his retirement<ref>{{cite web|website=featherstonerovers.co.uk|date=7 February 2019|access-date=26 December 2020|title=Brett Delaney announces retirement|url=https://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/news/brett-delaney-announces-retirement-pr9K4}}</ref>

==Representative career==
In 2008, Delaney was selected to represent [[New South Wales City rugby league team|NSW City]] in the annual [[City vs Country Origin|City vs Country Origin Game]]. He scored a try in the 22–22 draw.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

== Coaching career ==
=== Early career ===
After retiring from playing in early 2019, Delaney began coaching in the [[Leeds Rhinos]] youth system. He spent three years working with the scholarship and academy sides.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=1 February 2022 |title=Exclusive: Brett Delaney reveals his route to joining York Knights |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/exclusive-brett-delaney-reveals-his-route-to-joining-york-knights-47454/ |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> In 2021, he briefly worked as assistant coach to [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]] when his former Leeds coach joined [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="YEP York">{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=10 November 2021 |title=Brett Delaney: Leeds Rhinos legend joins York City Knights coaching staff |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rhinos-legend-delaney-joins-knights-coaching-staff-3453148 |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=11 November 2021 |title=York City Knights appoint Brett Delaney as Assistant Coach |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36257/york-city-knights-appoint-brett-delaney-as-assistant-coach |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=The RFL}}</ref>

Delaney joined the coaching staff at [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for the [[2022 York City Knights season|2022 season]], working as an assistant to [[James Ford (rugby league)|James Ford]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kilbride |first=Jacob |date=10 November 2021 |title=Brett Delaney joins York City Knights as assistant coach |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/19707877.brett-delaney-joins-york-city-knights-assistant-coach/ |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=The York Press}}</ref><ref name="YEP York" /> Partway through the year, he departed to take up a [[Super League]] opportunity at [[Hull KR]].<ref name="LRL York to HKR">{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=6 July 2022 |title=Brett Delaney leaves York for Super League opportunity with Hull KR |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brett-delaney-leaves-york-for-hull-kr-super-league |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
In July 2022, Delaney joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] as a forwards coach.<ref name="LRL York to HKR" /> He had previously agreed to join from the upcoming 2023 season under [[Willie Peters]], but made the move early following the interim appointment of [[Danny McGuire]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=6 July 2022 |title=Interim Hull KR head coach Danny McGuire enlists support of former Leeds Rhinos team-mate |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/interim-hull-kr-head-coach-danny-mcguire-enlists-support-of-former-leeds-rhinos-team-mate-3757567 |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=6 July 2022 |title=Delaney joins Robins coaching staff |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2865/delaney-joins-robins-coaching-staff |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Super League}}</ref> In April 2023, Delaney signed a two-year contract extension with Hull KR alongside fellow coach [[David Hodgson (rugby league)|David Hodgson]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Appleyard |first=Joe |date=22 April 2023 |title=Hull KR tie down pivotal coaching staff members with key traits explained |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brett-delaney-hull-kr-hodgson-8373192 |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Hull Daily Mail}}</ref>

During Delaney's time at Hull KR, the team's defensive record improved from 22.52 points conceded per game in 2022, to 12.07 in 2024.<ref name="HDM HKR to Cas">{{Cite web |last=Rej |first=Arindam |date=23 October 2024 |title=Brett Delaney's Hull KR exit is a loss but paves way for close friends' reunion |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brett-delaneys-hull-kr-exit-9656753 |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Hull Daily Mail}}</ref> In October 2024, he was linked with a move to [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=21 October 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers eye up Hull KR coaching swoop as Craig Lingard succession plans emerge |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-eye-up-hull-kr-coaching-swoop-as-craig-lingard-succession-plans-emerge |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> and his exit from Hull KR was announced on 23 October.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=23 October 2024 |title=Hull KR confirm Brett Delaney departure ahead of Danny McGuire reunion at Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brett-delaney-hull-kr-castleford-tigers-danny-mcguire-reunion |access-date=30 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref name="HDM HKR to Cas" />

=== Castleford Tigers ===
On 29 October 2024, [[Castleford Tigers]] confirmed that Delaney had been appointed assistant coach, working under new head coach [[Danny McGuire]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=29 October 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers: Brett Delaney teams up again with Danny McGuire after being appointed assistant head coach |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-brett-delaney-teams-up-again-with-danny-mcguire-after-being-appointed-assistant-head-coach-4845055 |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He would be reunited with McGuire, whom he had played and coached alongside previously, and would lead on defensive coaching and structures.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=29 October 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm Leeds Rhinos icon to join coaching staff in 2025 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-confirm-brett-delaney-coaching-staff |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=29 October 2024 |title=Why Danny McGuire thinks his new No 2 at Castleford Tigers is one of the best in the business |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/why-danny-mcguire-thinks-his-new-no-2-at-castleford-tigers-is-one-of-the-best-in-the-business-4845123 |access-date=29 October 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

On 27 May 2026, Castleford announced that Delaney had departed the club.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=27 May 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm second major departure as assistant coach leaves club |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-assistant-coach-leaves-1856902 |access-date=27 May 2026 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref>

==Honours==
===Club===
* [[Super League]] (4): [[Super League XVI|2011]], [[Super League XVII|2012]], [[Super League XX|2015]], [[Super League XXII|2017]]
* [[World Club Challenge]] (1): [[2012 World Club Challenge|2012]]
* [[League Leaders' Shield]] (1): [[Super League XX|2015]]
* [[Challenge Cup]] (2): [[Challenge Cup|2014]], [[Challenge Cup|2015]]
* [[Minor Premiership]] winner with [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] in 2005
